We will be back!

reviews.text:
My two girlfriends and I came in for lunch and had such a wonderful experience! It was our first time at Ciao Baby at lunch time, and the young Gentleman who served us was absolutely fantastic! He was friendly, attentive, and made us feel very welcome from the moment we arrived.
For dessert, he recommended the salted caramel gelato, and it was absolutely delicious 😋 we’re so glad we tried it! Everything about our visit was lovely, and we will definitely be coming back again soon!

reviews.text: A Really immersive experience that must be experienced. All aspects of the dining experience excelled. Our server Kelley was attentive and caring down to the last detail. A family run Italian restaurant that made you feel at home but with the richness of the finest Medditerranean chefs and cuisine. The food was amazing from the fresh bread and olive oil balsamic dip to the rich desserts and treats. For the ultimate taste of Italy you must come here and check this off your bucket list. 10 stars and 2 thumbs way up!!!!

reviews.text: I’ve been to Ciao Baby twice, and every time I I’ve visited Ciao Baby twice now, and every single time it’s an amazing experience. The food is absolutely exquisite — full of flavor, fresh, and beautifully presented. The service is exceptional; our waitress was so kind, always smiling, and made us feel genuinely welcome. This time I ordered pizza to go, and the chef personally came out to ask exactly how I wanted it prepared — such a thoughtful and professional gesture. Every detail shows how much they care about their customers. I truly love this restaurant and highly recommend it 100%. 10/10 — you must try it!

reviews.text:
Dear Friends, I dined-in at "Ciao Baby!" on E Main St (Lake Cook Rd) in Barrington recently and totally enjoyed the experience.  Let me give you the high points:
1. Parking was easy right on Main Street in front of the restaurant.
2. Great interior atmosphere, attractive tables w/ table cloths in a tastefully (pun intended) front and back dining room.  Wine bottles are cleverly arranged on the wall.  In the back dining room, Italian frescos were visable through the windows.
3. Food was delicious, I had the Chicken Parmesan.
4. The prices were reasonable AND they had generous portion sizes.  If you've read any of my other reviews, you'll know I like to take home part of my meal for another time.
5. Service was friendly, fast and efficient.  The wait staff apologized for not having Cannoli's on the dessert menu...ask for them anyways and maybe they'll get the message!!!
The $64,000 question is whether I'd return...yes, in a heart-beat.  If you've never been there, give the place a try and tell them that "Rael" sent you!
reviews.owner_reply:
Response from the owner a year ago
THANK YOU SO MUCH FOR YOUR VERY KIND WORDS.  WE WORK HARD AS A TEAM TO MAKE SURE OUR GUESTS HAVE THE BEST EXPERIENCE POSSIBLE AND TAKE PRIDE IN EVERYTHING WE DO. AND YES, WE WILL LOOK INTO THE CANNOLIS.
Bill
reviews.raw:
Rael Imperialak
Local Guide · 48 reviews · 23 photos


4. The prices were reasonable AND they had generous portion sizes.  If you've read any of my other reviews, you'll know I like to take home part of my meal for another time.

reviews.text: I dined in last night since my last visit June 2023! This dinner was even better! I had 2 appetizers: the Melrose Peppers and Vesuvio Lambchops (both were DELICIOUS)! I was going to have their world famous Meatloaf, but the very cool gentleman in the table next to me got the last one -- LOL. (Oh well...great excuse to come back! Anyway, I actually I had it in my last visit!) So, I had the Grouper Franchese (my other favorite entree), which was also SPECTACULARLY DELICIOUS! For dessert, I had the chocolate cake! It was the size of Greenland and HEAVENLY DELICIOUS!!! The restaurant was very relaxing to be in that night and had some really good chill music playing overhead (unlike the obnoxiously loud club music at Lago in Lake Zurich--where I no longer dine at) and had 1 other (later, 2 other) tables of customers dining near me--Wednesday night is definitely a great night to dine here! My server, Kelly, did a fantastic job taking care of me-- she was very upbeat, personable, knowledgeable and efficient! I will definitely be back!
reviews.owner_reply:
Response from the owner a year ago
Hi Jose, thank you for your kind words! Welcome  back! Bill
reviews.raw:
Jose Tolentino

reviews.text:
I'm Italian and have eaten a lot of Italian food. The food there was incredible. Everything from the bread, to the calamari, to the braciole. And the Merlot.
Excellent service. Attentive without being intrusive.
Sort of a nightclub atmosphere. U-shaped booths with leather upholstery.  Lots of black and white photos of celebrities like Sinatra, Bogart, etc. in clubs hanging on the walls.
Wish I had taken photos - next time!

reviews.text:
What a fabulous spot. We met two other couples for lunch and had a wonderful experience. The restaurant isn’t too crowded during the day and we were able to linger for a few hours chatting and never felt rushed. Our antipasta salad was huge. We shared it among 3 people because we couldn’t finish it all. $23 might seem steep for a salad but it was chock full of artichokes, cheese and various salumi. They brought warm bread to the table to accompany the pasta and salads ordered. The chicken sandwich looked amazing. Their carrot cake slice was enough for 6 to share.
We will definitely be back. Service was so friendly.
